Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, ketones), suggesting impaired renal function and possible nephrotic syndrome or glomerular disease. Dyslipidemia (high cholesterol, LDL, triglycerides) and mildly elevated liver enzymes are present. HbA1c is low, excluding diabetes. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for renal dysfunction and further assessment of possible underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
